title: Telanthophora guerkei
taxonomy: Telanthophora guerkei is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It is a member of the genus Telanthophora, which is a group of flowering plants endemic to the Falkland Islands. The plant is a herbaceous perennial that produces small flowers in shades of pink and purple. Its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae, the clade Angiosperms, and the order Asterales. The plant's specific classification helps to understand its evolutionary relationships and characteristics.

constituents: Telanthophora guerkei contains sesquiterpene lactones, which are known for their biological and pharmacological activities. These compounds are found in the plant's leaves and stems.
